text.tspan: Query 'fox' u2192 SCAN every doc u2192 O(docs u00d7 terms_per_doc). For 1B docs: hopeless.
Query 'fox' u2192 ONE lookup u2192 [doc1, doc3]. O(1) for the term.
Query 'quick AND fox' u2192 intersect [doc1, doc3] AND [doc1, doc3] = [doc1, doc3].
POSTINGS LISTS u2014 the 'list of doc_ids' part is rich:
u2022 term frequency (for relevance ranking: TF-IDF, BM25)
u2022 offsets (for highlighting matches in results)
Postings are stored sorted; intersected via merge-join for AND queries.
Compressed heavily (delta encoding, variable-byte, simd-bp128) u2014 inverted
indexes are often 10u201340% the size of the original text.
1. TOKENIZE: split text into terms ('Don't' u2192 'don', 't')
MySQL FULLTEXT, MongoDB text index, Algolia, Meilisearch, Tantivy,
Google's index (much fancier but same idea), Bing, Baidu, every search engine ever.
